content.problemStatement: Write a program that takes two numbers and an operator (+, -, *, /) as input. Use a **switch** statement to perform the corresponding arithmetic operation and print the result.
constraints: Division by zero will not appear in the input
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains two integers num1 and num2 (1 ≤ num1, num2 ≤ 1000) followed by a space and a character op which is one of '+', '-', '*', '/' .
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing the result of applying the operator op to num1 and num2. Division uses integer division (floor).

solution.approach: Read the number of test cases T. For each test case read num1, num2 and the operator character. Use a switch (or equivalent) to select the appropriate arithmetic operation: addition, subtraction, multiplication, or integer division. Print the computed result. All operations are O(1), so the total time is O(T). Memory usage is O(1).
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – each test case is processed in constant time.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables are stored.
